element_introduction_order.element: Driving aids (steering help, braking help, automatic turn-around)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players can disable aids progressively to increase challenge; higher difficulty levels restrict aid availability
Familiarization with circuit before competitive pressure
Grid position competition; introduces time pressure and competitive ranking
Full-length competition with AI opponents, mechanical failures, crashes

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available throughout but mastered over time
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: High-detail tuning system allowing players to optimize performance
Visual feedback (flames, smoke); cars remain partially operational before parking
Turn-based competitive racing with AI simulation of other players' cars
Real-time competitive play against human players over network
16-race circuit progression; cumulative scoring across all 1994 season venues

typical_relief_triggers: Save game feature enabling progress checkpoint
typical_tension_triggers: Championship mode cumulative pressure across 16 circuits
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation from practice (safe exploration) through qualifying (moderate competition) to racing (high intensity). Championship mode extends tension across entire season. Mechanical failures introduce unpredictable tension spikes during races.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Quickrace mode allows jumping to any circuit without progression; customizable lap counts and grid positions signal optional difficulty scaling

difficulty_curve.early_game: Practice sessions with full driving aids available (steering help, braking help, automatic turn-around, racing line help, traction control, automatic shifting). Indestructibility option permits consequence-free exploration.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Qualifying introduces competitive time pressure. Driving aids begin restriction based on chosen difficulty level (five available levels). Car setup optimization becomes strategically important.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship mode demands consistent performance across 16 circuits. Higher difficulty levels severely restrict or eliminate driving aids. Mechanical failures introduce unreliable challenge. AI opponents operate at peak capability.
notable_spikes: Transition from practice to qualifying (introduction of time pressure and ranking)
First race with mechanical failures enabled (unpredictable crisis management)
Championship mode final circuits (cumulative fatigue pressure against consistent AI)
Difficulty level 5 with minimal/no driving aids (skill ceiling)

notable_level_patterns.description: 16 distinct Formula One circuits from 1994 season, each with unique spatial layouts, safety configurations (some reflect mid-season updates), and strategic racing lines. Circuits vary in difficulty and driver skill requirements.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Silverstone (includes safety-related changes made during 1994 season); Barcelona (missing safety updates, representing earlier season state)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each circuit follows realistic Formula One event structure. Practice allows low-pressure familiarization. Qualifying introduces competition for grid position. Race delivers full challenge. Quickrace bypasses structure for immediate racing.

First racing game to visually simulate mechanical failures. Engine/gearbox/electronic failures trigger visual feedback (flames, smoke) without immediate race exit. Cars remain partially operational for limited time before pit parking. Creates dynamic difficulty variance.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any race with mechanical failures enabled

notable_level_patterns.description: Five difficulty levels control access to seven driving aids. Lower difficulties permit all aids (safe learning). Higher difficulties restrict aid availability progressively (moderate to dangerous). Indestructibility toggle separates penalty-free exploration from consequential racing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any circuit at different difficulty levels
